3A_Bibliographic.citation: Nagata, J. M., Cortez, C. A., Cattle, C. J., et al. (2022). Screen time use among US adolescents during the COVID-19 pandemic: Findings from the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) Study. JAMA Pediatrics, 176(1), 94-96.
3A_Bibliographic.senior_author: UNKNOWN - PDF only contains supplementary materials
3A_Bibliographic.notes: PDF file only contains the Supplementary Online Content (eMethods, eReferences), not the main article text. Coded from available supplementary content only. The main article (a Research Letter) is not in this PDF.

3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: The included subsample (n=5,412) retained a higher proportion of female, White, Asian, high-income, and higher parent education participants compared to those excluded due to nonresponse or missing data (n=6,463).
3B_Sample_Data.notes: Only supplementary materials available. Main article text is missing from the PDF.

3C_Exposure.exposure_variable: Screen time during COVID-19 pandemic (total and by 7 modalities)
3C_Exposure.exposure_instrument: ABCD COVID-19 survey screen time questions
3C_Exposure.modalities: single-player video games, multiplayer video games, texting, social media apps, video chat, searching/browsing internet, watching/streaming movies/videos/TV shows
3C_Exposure.operationalization: Continuous; response options from 0 min to 24 hrs; total screen time calculated by summing average screen time across seven modalities
3C_Exposure.time_frame: Average day in the past week (during COVID-19)
3C_Exposure.verbatim_exposure_description: The instructions for questions in this section specified, 'Please do NOT include time spent on school related work.' Participants were asked, 'On an average day in the past week, how much time do you:' [seven screen activities listed]. Response options included 0 min, 15 mins, 30 mins, 45 mins, 1 hr, 1.5 hrs, 2 hrs, 2.5 hrs, 3 hrs and every additional hour up until 24 hrs. Total screen time was calculated by summing average screen time of these seven modalities.
3C_Exposure.notes: Only supplement available. Main article contains the primary results which are not accessible from this PDF.

3D_Outcome.primary_outcome: Screen time during COVID-19 (descriptive); associations with mental health, COVID-related worry, perceived stress, social support, coping behaviors
3D_Outcome.outcome_instrument: Single-item mental health measure, COVID-Related Worry (5 items), Perceived Stress Scale (4 items), social support (2 items), coping behaviors (9 items)
3D_Outcome.verbatim_outcome_description: Participants were asked, 'How do you think your mental health (emotional well-being) is in the past week compared to normal?' Responses included: 1 = 'much worse'; 2 = 'a little worse'; 3 = 'about the same'; 4 = 'a little better'; 5 = 'much better.'
3D_Outcome.notes: Only supplementary methods available. The main article (a Research Letter) contains the actual results/effect sizes which are not in this PDF.
